sanskrit:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये।
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः॥
transliteration: manuṣyāṇāṃ sahasreṣu kaścid yatati siddhaye | yatatām api siddhānāṃ kaścin māṃ vetti tattvataḥ ||
literal_meaning: Among thousands of humans, one strives for perfection; even among those who strive and attain perfection, hardly one knows Me in truth.
contextual_meaning: The rarity of genuine self-transcendence: statistical scarcity of sustained effort toward inner perfection, and an even rarer existential recognition of the Ground of Being as a relational reality rather than an abstract principle.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance between the ego's wish for quick enlightenment and the data-driven reality that authentic realization is extraordinarily rare; humility induction.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling past thousands of self-proclaimed gurus on social media, realizing how few have done the disciplined inner work, and feeling both humbled and motivated to deepen one's own practice.
anchor_text: True knowing begins when you seek the One behind every face.
